name: 4-Way Conflict Resolution
overview: TrustDB's conflict resolution classifies sync conflicts into 4 categories (clean, convergent, divergent, novel), routes divergent conflicts through a confidence-weighted resolution pipeline, and archives all conflict decisions with snapshot provenance. A clearance-enforced conflict queue ensures only authorized personnel can resolve governance-sensitive conflicts.

howItWorks.title: 4-Way Classification
howItWorks.description: During sync, each entity change is classified: Clean (no remote change), Convergent (identical change on both sides), Divergent (conflicting changes), or Novel (new entity created offline). Classification uses content hashing and version vector comparison.

howItWorks.title: Automatic Resolution
howItWorks.description: Clean and convergent conflicts resolve automatically (accept local or either version). Novel entities are accepted with their offline-penalized confidence. Only divergent conflicts require resolution logic.

howItWorks.title: Conflict Queue
howItWorks.description: Divergent conflicts that cannot be auto-resolved (confidence scores within tie-break threshold, or governance-classified data) are routed to a conflict queue. The queue is clearance-enforced — only users with appropriate governance clearance can view and resolve conflicts in their classification domain.

howItWorks.title: Snapshot Archive
howItWorks.description: Every conflict resolution decision is archived with: both conflicting versions, the resolution choice, resolver identity, timestamp, and a snapshot of the governance context at resolution time. This provides full auditability of all conflict decisions.

comparisons.narrative: Most distributed databases offer limited conflict resolution: last-write-wins (DynamoDB, Cassandra) or application-level callbacks (CouchDB). TrustDB's 4-way classification with governance-aware routing is more sophisticated but also more complex to reason about. The clearance-enforced conflict queue is unique — no other system restricts who can resolve conflicts based on data classification.

competitors.limitation: Silently discards losing writes; no conflict visibility; no governance awareness
Application must implement resolution logic; no confidence-based auto-resolution
Timestamp-based resolution only; no conflict queue; no clearance enforcement
Siblings must be resolved by application; no confidence or governance integration
